title: santolina chamaecyparissus var. brevicaulis
intro: Santolina chamaecyparissus var. brevicaulis, also known as Short-leaf Grey Wormwood or Short-leaved Cotton Lavender, is a perennial shrub native to the Mediterranean region, characterized by its greyish-green foliage and yellowish flowers. The plant has been used in herbal medicine for its antimicrobial, antifungal, and antiseptic properties, making it effective in treating wounds, skin infections, and respiratory issues. The health benefits of Santolina chamaecyparissus var. brevicaulis include its ability to soothe digestive issues, reduce inflammation, and aid in the treatment of colds and flu. The plant can be used in herbal preparations such as teas, tinctures, and salves, allowing for its medicinal properties to be harnessed for a variety of health benefits.
